 {"id":"bd-h9lpq","title":"[HIGH] 16 KiB btrfs readdir fixture bypasses the shared checksum helper and drifts from parser semantics","description":"# Review finding

The remaining red btrfs directory-walk family is the only synthetic-tree fixture in `ffs-core` still hand-rolling tree-block checksum logic instead of going through the shared test helper.

## Evidence
- Shared helper exists at `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:22328-22332`: `stamp_btrfs_test_tree_block_crc32c(image, logical)`.
- That helper is used by the green 4 KiB synthetic-tree fixtures (`crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:22357-22561`, `23010-23011`).
- `build_btrfs_readdir_image()` instead inlines its own checksum math at `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:23372-23375` while also switching the superblock `nodesize` to `16384`.
- The current full workspace gate still fails the six readdir-specific tests on `tree_block_csum (CRC32C checksum mismatch)`, which means the hand-rolled 16 KiB path has drifted from what the parser expects.

## Why this is high severity
This is now the only remaining workspace-red btrfs fixture family after the default-root smoke fix landed. Keeping a second checksum implementation in test builders is actively hiding the real contract and slowing down recovery.

## Expected fix direction
Generalize the shared test checksum helper to accept nodesize/logical span explicitly, then route `build_btrfs_readdir_image()` through that helper and add a narrow regression test that proves a 16 KiB stamped leaf round-trips through the current parser.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:12:53Z","created_by":"cod-fs","updated_at":"2026-04-22T20:36:14Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T20:36:14Z","close_reason":"Already shipped; targeted btrfs readdir repros now pass","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","ffs-core","fixtures","qa","review","workspace-gate"]}
+{"id":"bd-haq43","title":"EXT4_IOC_CLEAR_ES_CACHE ioctl: drop ext4 extent status cache","description":"EXT4_IOC_CLEAR_ES_CACHE = _IO(0x66, 40) = 0x6628 is the kernel side knob that tells ext4 to drop the in-memory extent status (es) cache for an inode. e2fsprogs and certain debug/ops tools issue it before re-reading extent state from disk to defeat caching artifacts (e.g. after offline metadata edits via debugfs). Pre-fix dispatch_ioctl returns ENOTTY because the FUSE handler has no branch.

Implementation plan:
- ffs-core: add `FsOps::clear_extent_status_cache(cx, scope, ino) -> Result<()>` trait method (default Ok(())).
- OpenFs override: ext4 reads the inode (validates existence) and returns Ok — FrankenFS keeps extent state per-scope (RequestScope-bound), not in a long-lived inode-level cache, so there is nothing to drop. btrfs returns Ok after reading the inode (it has no equivalent es cache; btrfs FIEMAP walks extent_data items inline each time).
- ffs-fuse: const EXT4_IOC_CLEAR_ES_CACHE = 0x6628. dispatch_ioctl branch routes through `ops.clear_extent_status_cache(...)` under RequestOp::IoctlRead scope. No input/output payload (it is _IO with size=0).
- IoctlRecordingFs gains an IoctlCall::ClearEsCache(InodeNumber) trace variant + matching FsOps override.
- Tests: dispatch routes to FsOps with empty reply; ignores any input payload + non-zero out_size per _IO contract.

Acceptance:
- ENOTTY -> Ok(empty reply) round trip via FUSE ioctl path.
- Trace asserts Begin(IoctlRead) + ClearEsCache(ino) + End(IoctlRead).
- Backend errors (eg invalid inode read) surface as ioctl errno not stale 0.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T07:38:46.846418274Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T09:02:43.033353170Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T09:02:43.031062362Z","close_reason":"EXT4_IOC_CLEAR_ES_CACHE dispatch + ext4/btrfs scope-based no-op + 2 dispatch regression tests; 287 ffs-fuse + 708 ffs-core green","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-hei34","title":"add mounted-path ext4 SEEK_DATA/SEEK_HOLE edge-case coverage for fully-allocated and leading-hole files","description":"Survey finding: fuse_e2e currently verifies ext4 SEEK_DATA/SEEK_HOLE only for a middle punched-hole layout, while ffs-core also implements fully-allocated and leading-hole semantics. Scope: add mounted-path ext4 probes that freeze SEEK_HOLE on a fully allocated file returning the virtual EOF hole, and SEEK_DATA/SEEK_HOLE on a file with a leading sparse hole returning the next data offset and hole start correctly, including EOF ENXIO behavior. Acceptance: ext4 FUSE E2E covers these two additional sparse-layout edge cases with exact offsets.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:10:32.731277659Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T18:47:30.900657933Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T18:47:30.898786171Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-hfu1","title":"[CLI] Harden mkfs output path + label reporting","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-11T17:37:16.371654870Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-11T17:41:15.386703993Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T17:41:15.386363849Z","close_reason":"Harden mkfs output validation and label reporting","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-hks","title":"ffs-block: Plan write-back cache + dirty tracking (phased)","description":"Future-facing task: define how cached writes become durable.

Deliverables:
- DirtyTracker design: which blocks are dirty, ordering constraints, flush scheduling.
- Interaction with MVCC: versioned writes vs overwriting.
- Failure/cancellation semantics (fsync, flush on close, etc).

Acceptance:
- A concrete design exists in docs/spec before any write-back code is added.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:16:07.481262948Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T03:48:46.342359407Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T03:48:46.342335122Z","close_reason":"Design doc created at docs/design-writeback-cache.md with 3-phase plan: DirtyTracker, FlushDaemon, MVCC integration","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["io"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-hks","depends_on_id":"bd-220","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:16:27.218279466Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hks","depends_on_id":"bd-5iz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:16:27.135355880Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
